hub_subtitle: Business English para Missões Internacionais e Pequenos Negócios

lesson.menu_desc: Talk about your health and daily habits with frequency expressions -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 10 -- Wellness & Routines
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Flávia Florêncio Ferreira | Lesson 10 -- Wellness & Routines
lesson.grammar_point: frequency expressions (once a week, twice a day, every morning)

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
the good state of your body and mind
b
physical activity you do to stay strong and fit
c
the rest you get when you close your eyes at night
d
the food you eat at one time, like breakfast or lunch
e
something you do often, almost without thinking
f
the power your body has to do things
g
a feeling of worry or pressure
h
a healthy mix between work and rest
i
to become calm and let go of worry
j
to stop and give your body a short break

paras: Flavia is a consultant, and she has a busy life. But this year, she takes care of her health. She has a simple plan for wellness, with good habits every day. She wants more energy and less stress at work.

'Every morning, I drink a glass of water and I do exercise for twenty minutes,' she says. 'I eat three meals a day, and I never skip breakfast. Twice a day, I stop and I rest for ten minutes to relax. I get eight hours of sleep every night.'

Flavia also finds a good balance between work and rest. Three times a week, she walks in the park with a friend. Once a month, she takes a full day to relax. These small habits give her more energy, and she feels healthy and calm. Good health helps her work with confidence.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about a simple weekly wellness routine and healthy habits

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Flavia is sick and cannot work this year.
Flavia follows simple habits every week to stay healthy and have more energy.
The text explains how to cook three meals in a restaurant.

items: This year, Flavia takes care of her health.
The text says: 'this year, she takes care of her health.'
Every morning, Flavia does exercise for twenty minutes.
The text says: 'Every morning, I ... do exercise for twenty minutes.'
Flavia sleeps only four hours every night.
The text says: 'I get eight hours of sleep every night.'
Once a month, Flavia takes a full day to relax.
The text says: 'Once a month, she takes a full day to relax.'

for twenty minutes. She eats three
a day and never skips breakfast. She sleeps eight hours every
, so she has a lot of

gapfill.text: Frequency expressions tell us how often: once / twice / three times a week, and every morning / day / night. They usually go at the end of the sentence.

bank.label: Useful sentences to talk about your wellness routine
items: I take care of my health every day.
Every morning, I do exercise for twenty minutes.
I eat three meals a day and I drink water.
Twice a day, I rest and relax for a few minutes.
I get eight hours of sleep every night.
Three times a week, I walk in the park.
I need a good balance between work and rest.
These habits give me more energy and less stress.

lf.title: How often? Choose the frequency expression
items: morning, I drink a glass of water.
a day, after lunch and after work.
, I take a full day to relax.

lf.text: Use once / twice / three times + a + period for a number of times, and every + morning / day / week for each one.

items.situation: Say one healthy habit you do every day.
tips: Every morning, I drink a glass of water.

items.situation: Say how often you exercise. Use a frequency expression.
tips: I do exercise three times a week.
I walk in the park twice a week.

items.situation: Say what you do to relax during the day.
tips: Twice a day, I rest and relax for ten minutes.
Every evening, I relax after work.

items.situation: Say how many hours you sleep. Use 'every night.'
tips: I get eight hours of sleep every night.
I sleep seven hours every night.

items: Describe your morning routine in three sentences. Use 'every morning' and one more frequency expression.
Ask a partner two questions with 'How often...?' about exercise and sleep, then answer one about your own habits.
Describe your whole wellness week: your habits, how often you do them, and how they help your energy and stress.
A friend asks: 'How do you find balance between work and rest?' Answer with two habits and how often you do them.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Model routine: 'Every morning, I drink water and do exercise for twenty minutes.'
how often: 'I walk in the park three times a week, and I rest twice a day.'
sleep and meals: 'I get eight hours of sleep every night, and I eat three meals a day.'
balance: 'These habits give me more energy and less stress, so I feel healthy.'
answerkey.note: put the frequency expression at the end: three times a week, twice a day, every morning / night.

listenings.text: Hi, this is Flavia. Let me tell you about my healthy week. Every morning, I do exercise for twenty minutes and I drink a glass of water. I eat three meals a day, and I never skip breakfast. Twice a day, I stop and I rest for ten minutes to relax. I get eight hours of sleep every night. These small habits give me more energy and less stress. Try one new habit this week!

Good morning, and welcome to the Healthy Life podcast. Here is a simple tip for a better week. First, drink water every morning. Second, do exercise at least three times a week. Third, take a short break twice a day to rest and relax. And remember, sleep is very important, so get seven or eight hours every night. Small habits make a big difference. Have a healthy week!

extra_audio.text: First, every morning, Flavia drinks a glass of water and does exercise. Then she eats a healthy breakfast before work. Twice a day, she rests for ten minutes to relax. In the evening, she takes a short walk to lower her stress. Finally, she gets eight hours of sleep every night.
